rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN REALTY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 02, 2014 line 1. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFF CALENDAR. The entire action was dismissed June 30, 2014. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD VS. WILLIAM B. REILLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Exhibit And Index Table
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 02, 2014 line 2.CROSS DEFENDANT 1369 HYDE STREET, LTD's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication And Exhibit And Index Table Summary Judgment is DENIED. Declarations establish cross-complainants were not aware of the video camera until 2011 and their unawareness was reasonable, making their November 2012 cross-complaint timely under the two-year statute of limitations. See Declarations of William, Meghan and Barbara Reilly at 2-9. Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to COAs 1, 2, & 3. Reasonable expectation of privacy is joint issue of law and fact and landlord fails to show no possible reasonable expectation of privacy from surreptitious video recording in common areas and through open door of apartment. Separate statements show dispute of material fact about placement of camera and degree to which camera recorded images of the inside of the rental unit. See Declarations of William, Meghan and Barbara Reilly at 2-9. Summary Adjudication is GRANTED as to COA 4 because § 1708.8(b) only creates liability when the image taken "could not have been achieved without a trespass unless the visual or auditory enhancing device was used." Here, the landlord was authorized to be in the public area in front of the door and could have looked into the open door himself, so 1708.8(b) doesn't apply. Summary Adjudication as to COA 5 is DENIED. Motion does not establish personal information in question had a connection or logical relation to the action, such that the litigation privilege would apply. See Silberg v. Anderson, 50 Cal.3d 205, 212. = (501/REQ)
